# breeze stage cancel is the manual escape hatch for a stuck Running/Awaiting
# instance (e.g. one left behind by a daemon restart mid-run, see
# TestShutdownCancelsRunningStages for the automatic case this complements) --
# force it to a terminal, retryable Failed state.
#
# Known limitation this test deliberately works around: cancel only mutates
# TRACKED state, it doesn't kill an actually-still-alive underlying process (no
# live handle to it exists to cancel) -- if the real command is still genuinely
# running (unlike the restart-orphaned case, where it's already gone) and later
# completes on its own, that real result can still land and overwrite the
# cancellation. The build command here never finishes within the test's
# lifetime for exactly this reason -- it's killed outright at the end instead.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token
exec breeze identity register ci
cp stdout ci.token
exec breeze role assign builder ci --as admin --token-file admin.token
exec breeze apply -f pipeline.hcl --as admin --token-file admin.token

# Start a slow build in the background, wait for it to actually reach Running.
exec breeze stage start release build abc123 --as ci --token-file ci.token &build&
exec sleep 0.3
exec breeze stage status release build abc123 --json
stdout '"status":\s*"running"'

# ci (holds "builder", the role gating this stage) cancels the stuck run.
exec breeze stage cancel release build abc123 --as ci --reason stuck-cancelling --token-file ci.token
stdout 'build: failed \(cancelled\)'

exec breeze stage status release build abc123 --json
stdout '"status":\s*"failed"'
stdout '"error":\s*"stuck-cancelling"'

# Now retriggerable — the whole point of clearing a stuck instance.
exec breeze stage start release build newcommit --as ci --token-file ci.token
stdout 'build: succeeded'

kill
exec breeze stop

-- pipeline.hcl --
pipeline "release" {
  stage "build" {
    type          = "command"
    required_role = "builder"
    timeout       = "30s"
    command       = ["/bin/sh", "-c", "case \"$0\" in abc123) sleep 300 ;; *) exit 0 ;; esac", "{commit}"]
  }
}
